value.name: Vision Transformer (ViT)
value.description: An image is split into fixed-size patches, which are linearly embedded and concatenated with a learnable class token. Learnable 1D position embeddings are added to this sequence, which is then processed by a standard Transformer encoder consisting of multi-head self-attention and MLP blocks with residual connections and layer normalization.
value.key_formula: z_0 = [x_class; x^1_p E; ...; x^N_p E] + E_pos
value.novelty_vs_prior: Unlike prior work that combines CNNs with attention or uses specialized attention patterns for efficiency, ViT applies a vanilla Transformer architecture directly to patch sequences with minimal modification, relying on large-scale data rather than architectural inductive biases.

value.name: Transformer Encoder
value.description: Standard Transformer encoder layers consisting of Multi-Head Self-Attention (MSA) followed by an MLP block, with LayerNorm applied before each sub-layer and residual connections after each sub-layer.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Original proposal by Vaswani et al. (2017) for machine translation.

value.name: Hybrid Architecture
value.description: A method where the input sequence to the Transformer is formed from feature maps of a CNN (e.g., ResNet) instead of raw image patches, potentially with a patch size of 1x1 corresponding to the spatial dimensions of the feature map.
value.novelty_vs_prior: Combining CNN backbones with Transformers has been explored in prior works (e.g., Wang et al., 2018; Wu et al., 2020); this paper implements it as a variant of ViT.
